arts.ttl: Daily Schmankerl: Bayern Munich’s Max Eberl talks tough decisions amid strategic planning; Manchester City views Florian Wirtz as “dream option”; Manchester United to overhaul roster?; Bayern to sell right-back?; Real Madrid wants midfielder; and MORE!
arts.sum: Bayern Munich board member for sport Max Eberl saw the most recent report on potential contract extensions for Leroy Sané, Sven Ulreich, and Eric Dier, plus a multitude of rumors involving other players including Leon Goretzka. The “dream” option for Manchester City this summer is Bayer Leverkusen attacking midfielder Florian Wirtz, reports Fabrizio Romano.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s Harry Kane talks about Jamal Musiala, his incredible growth and the high levels he can still reach
arts.sum: He’s one of the best I’ve played with,” Kane said, as captured by @iMiaSanMia . Fast forward to 2025, however, Musiala and Kane are two of Bayern Munich ’s biggest stars and ripping up defenses together.

arts.ttl: Serge Gnabry wants to repeat UCL triumph from 2020 in the Allianz Arena
arts.sum: That included the conclusion of the 2020 Champions League, which saw Bayern Munich triumph against Paris Saint Germain to lift the big eared trophy...in front of no fans. Gnabry, an important member of the 2020 Champions League winning squad, talks about how much of a dream such a scenario would be.

arts.ttl: With Leon Goretzka and Harry Kane set to play vs. Inter Milan tilt in Champions League, Bayern Munich Joshua Kimmich calls for everyone to step up
arts.sum: Bayern Munich midfielder Joshua Kimmich is putting out the call — everyone on the squad is vital to the tasks at hand and all will be needed for the team to achieve its ultimate goals. Harry Kane (ankle knock) and Leon Goretzka (back problems) trained with the team today and should both be ready to start against Inter on Tuesday.

arts.ttl: Players stream in with appreciation, thanks for Thomas Müller’s career, impact at Bayern Munich
arts.sum: As one might expect, Bayern Munich fans are not the only ones hitting social media to voice their support for and appreciation of outgoing club legend Thomas Müller. Bayern youngsters paying tribute to Thomas Müller pic.twitter.com/UsjgQQhpA8

arts.ttl: What did Bayern Munich do right before they decided against giving Thomas Müller an extension?
arts.sum: Bayern Munich backtracking on their intent to extend club legend Thomas Müller has fans feeling surprised, confused, hurt, angry, disappointed, and the like. “We had our first conversation with Thomas in mid-March, and the decisions about squad planning were made at the beginning of March,” Max Eberl said (Sport1-Doppelpass via @iMiaSanMia ).

arts.ttl: Inter making progress in move for Marseille’s Luis Henrique
arts.sum: While the Serie A title race as well as the Champions League knockouts have Inter occupied, they continue to have one eye on the transfer market. The latest reports via…

arts.ttl: Max Eberl maintains that the decision to not extend Thomas Müller was a sporting decision and not a financial one
arts.sum: Max Eberl then went out and said that the final nail in Müller’s Bayern career was a sporting decision and that it had nothing to do with finances. I was emotional at the time and couldn’t imagine FC Bayern without Thomas Müller.”

arts.ttl: Max Eberl admits to crucial mistake in the events that transpired in the Thomas Müller-Bayern Munich separation
arts.sum: For context, Eberl was quoted as saying in January about Müller: “He doesn’t need to negotiate much. I understand that Thomas went into the talks with that feeling and that he was disappointed afterwards,” Eberl revealed (Sport1-Doppelpass as captured by X account @iMiaSanMia ).
